risk_name: PEM Stack Degradation Exceeding Warranty Rate Under Dynamic Operation

description.summary: PEM stacks operated under highly variable renewable profiles degrade at 1.5-2.0%/year versus the warranted 1.0%/year, leading to higher electricity consumption and early stack replacement (year 5-6 instead of year 8-10).
description.detailed: PEM electrolyzer stacks degrade through multiple mechanisms: membrane thinning, catalyst dissolution, bipolar plate corrosion, and membrane electrode assembly delamination. Dynamic operation (frequent start-stop, rapid ramping, partial load) accelerates degradation beyond the steady-state rate used for OEM warranty specifications. Observed degradation rates of 1.5-2.0%/year in real-world dynamic operation vs 1.0%/year warranted create a 0.5-1.0%/year gap. At 100 MW scale, each 0.5% additional degradation rate translates to ~€200,000/year in additional electricity cost, compounding over the project life. Early stack replacement at 40,000-50,000 hours instead of 60,000-80,000 adds €15-25M unplanned CAPEX. OEM warranty disputes are emerging as a significant issue in early commercial PEM plants.
description.root_cause: Dynamic operation (renewable intermittency) accelerates degradation mechanisms beyond steady-state lab testing conditions used for warranty specifications. OEMs have limited field data for >20 MW plants with >3 years dynamic operation.
trigger_events: Annual efficiency audit shows >1.2%/year degradation rate
EIS monitoring detects accelerating membrane resistance trend
Individual cell voltage monitoring identifies >5% cells operating above 2.0V
OEM notifies warranty exclusion due to operational profile

assessment.probability_rationale: Observed in 3 of 18 reference PEM projects >20 MW with >3 years operating data (~17% frequency). TC-PEM-001 TCR-PEM-001 documents as moderate probability.
assessment.impact_rationale: Major: €15-25M unplanned stack replacement CAPEX + 5-10% higher electricity OPEX over project life. Schedule impact: 4-6 week outage per stack module replacement.
assessment.detectability_rationale: EIS monitoring provides 12-18 month early warning before irreversible degradation. Individual cell voltage monitoring detects anomalies within weeks. High detectability with proper monitoring investment.
assessment.assessment_method: Technology Card evidence + IEA Electrolyser Durability 2025 + Gold Dataset project evidence

schedule.description: 4-6 weeks per stack module replacement. N+1 redundancy allows production to continue during replacement.

cost.description: €20M P50 for early stack replacement at 100 MW scale + €2-5M/year additional electricity cost at 1.5%/year degradation vs 1.0% warranted.

performance.description: Progressive efficiency loss of 1.5-2.0%/year vs 1.0% warranted. By year 10, plant may consume 5-10% more electricity per kg H₂ than financial model assumes.

preventive_actions.description: Negotiate degradation guarantee ≤1.0%/year for first 5 years, ≤1.5%/year through year 10, with defined operational profile boundaries
Implement EIS-based predictive monitoring with quarterly diagnostic testing

corrective_actions.description: Design plant with N+1 stack redundancy. Size BOP for full capacity even with one module offline.
Establish stack replacement sinking fund: allocate €0.05/kg H₂ into reserved account from year 1.

incident_descriptions: Puertollano (GA-PR-006): 20 MW PEM operational since 2022. Accumulating real-world degradation data under solar-coupled dynamic operation in Spanish climate. First multi-year operational dataset for PEM at this scale. Results are being monitored by the industry.

REFHYNE I (10 MW precursor to GA-PR-008 REFHYNE II): Operational since 2021 at Shell Wesseling. Provided degradation data that informed REFHYNE II specifications and warranty negotiations with ITM Power.
evidence.lessons_learned: Real-world degradation often exceeds lab data due to dynamic operation. EIS monitoring provides 12-18 months early warning. OEM warranties typically exclude degradation caused by 'excessive cycling' — operational profile must be negotiated upfront. Stack replacement sinking fund is essential — do not depend solely on warranty. N+1 redundancy adds ~8% CAPEX but eliminates production loss during stack swaps.
evidence.frequency_observed: Observed in ~17% of reference PEM projects >20 MW with >3 years data (3 of 18 projects)

sources.title: IEA Electrolyser Durability and Degradation: Lessons from First Commercial Plants, 2025
sources.author: IEA
